mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp acceleration in payroll growth above 150k jobs per month signals tight labor market conditions that drive corporate profit margins upward, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns remain negative when PAYEMS exceeds 150k for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent decline in housing starts and consumer confidence indices signals weakening demand conditions that depress S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive when both HOUST and CPIAUCSL decline simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production above its 3-month moving average indicates expanding real economic activity that drives S&P 500 returns positively over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative despite INDPRO rising above its 3-month MA.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence between rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) and stagnant monetary policy (FEDFUNDS) creates real rate uncertainty that depresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive when CPIAUCSL exceeds FEDFUNDS by 200 bps.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous drop in unemployment rate and rise in real compensation (RSAFS) signals strong labor market dynamics that boost corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls > 0.5pp and RSAFS rises > 1% in 1 month
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative when both UNRATE declines and RSAFS increases.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in GDP growth below 2% annualized signals deteriorating economic fundamentals that drive S&P 500 returns negative over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive despite GDPC1 growth below 2%.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production combined with declining capacity utilization signals overheating supply chains that pressure equity returns negatively over 20 days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises > 2% and UNRATE falls > 0.3pp in 3 months
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive when INDPRO rises and UNRATE falls simultaneously.
